name: Multi-Agent SOC — 9 Parallel AI Security Agents
description: APEX Multi-Agent SOC: 9 specialist AI agents (CVE Intel, IOC Hunter, SIEM Defender, Threat Hunter, IR Playbook, Compliance Guardian, Red Team, Zero Trust Sentinel, Risk Synthesizer) dispatched in parallel against a free-text task, with CVE/KEV/IOC context enrichment, real-time SSE streaming, and an executive risk-synthesis brief.

routes_sampled.evidence: workers/src/index.js:1955-1958 (path.startsWith prefix dispatch, agent_id read via path.split('/')[4])

frontend.evidence: frontend/soc-agents.html calls all 4 routes directly (fetch('/api/agents/stream') :427, fetch('/api/agents/run') :542, fetch('/api/agents/dispatch/...') :627,674, fetch('/api/agents/status') :699). frontend/index.html does NOT call any /api/agents/* route directly (0 matches) — it only links out to /soc-agents from the primary desktop nav (frontend/index.html:1142), mobile drawer (:1183), a homepage feature card (:5599-5609), and the flagship products grid (:5645). 6 other pages (frontend/mssp.html, frontend/god-mode.html, frontend/ai-red-team.html, frontend/soc-operations.html, frontend/attack-surface-management.html, frontend/threat-hunting.html, frontend/vulnerability-management.html) also link to /soc-agents; frontend/soc-dashboard.html and frontend/enterprise-portal.html embed only the GET /api/agents/status widget, not run/stream/dispatch.

navigation.evidence: Prominent placement in the primary desktop nav (frontend/index.html:1142, styled as a pill) and mobile drawer (:1183), plus a homepage feature card and 8 other product pages' cross-links. The /soc-agents target resolves via Cloudflare Pages' native Clean-URL lookup (frontend/_redirects, no explicit rewrite needed) to the real, present frontend/soc-agents.html — confirmed not a dead CTA.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/multiAgentSOC.test.mjs imports classifyTask, fetchCVEContext, fetchKEVStatus, fetchIOCContext, runAgent, handleAgentsStatus, handleAgentsRun, handleAgentDispatch, and (as of the 2026-07-08 SSE CORS fix) handleAgentsStream directly from workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js — all 9 entry_points now have real describe()-block coverage. handleAgentsStream's new 'SSE CORS' describe block asserts the 3 previously-rejected production origins (cyberdudebivash.pages.dev, tools.cyberdudebivash.com, intel.cyberdudebivash.com) are now reflected in Access-Control-Allow-Origin, plus the primary-origin and fail-closed-on-unknown-origin cases. fetchIOCContext is imported but never called in any it() block — imported, not exercised. workers/test/copilotAdvancedCapabilities.test.mjs:287-318 tests the AI-Copilot's use of this capability but does so against a full vi.doMock() of workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js, not the real module.

verification.evidence: FIXED this pass (dedicated change, own commit, own regression test — per docs/capability-registry/PROGRAM_BOARD.md's recommendation to not bundle it into a process/documentation PR): workers/src/index.js run/stream/dispatch route blocks (~1945-1968) now call `if (!isRealUser(authCtx)) return withSecurityHeaders(withCors(unauthorized(), request));` immediately after resolveAuthV5, using the identical established pattern already in place at 30+ other routes (e.g. the MFA block immediately above it) — no new middleware, no duplicated authorization logic. status is deliberately left ungated: it is read-only registry/health data with no compute cost, embedded unauthenticated in the public SOC dashboard widget (frontend/soc-dashboard.html:539, frontend/enterprise-portal.html), and both frontend consumers of the gated routes (frontend/soc-agents.html) already shipped with dormant 401-handling (handleUnauth(), :387-393, and per-call `if (resp.status===401)` checks at :434,544,628,675) that had never been exercised until now — this fix requires zero frontend changes. Regression coverage added: workers/test/authGateRealUser.test.mjs now asserts anonymous POST /api/agents/{run,stream,dispatch/:id} → 401, admin key → not-401 for run and dispatch, and anonymous GET /api/agents/status stays non-401. Full suite re-run green: 177 files / 1862 tests (npx vitest run, 2026-07-08). SECOND FIX, same day, own commit: the SSE CORS finding (item 1 in notes, below) is now also fixed — workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js's handleAgentsStream now imports `corsHeaders` from workers/src/middleware/cors.js and uses it in place of the hand-rolled `origin.endsWith('.cyberdudebivash.in')` check, so the 3 previously-rejected real production origins (cyberdudebivash.pages.dev, tools.cyberdudebivash.com, intel.cyberdudebivash.com) now get SSE streaming. New regression coverage: workers/test/multiAgentSOC.test.mjs's 'handleAgentsStream() — SSE CORS' describe block (5 cases: the 3 previously-broken origins now reflected, the primary origin still reflected, an unrecognized origin still fails closed to the primary origin). Full suite re-run green: 177 files / 1867 tests (npx vitest run, 2026-07-08). Prior static-verification evidence for the original findings retained below for audit trail.

notes: FIXED 2026-07-08 (see verification.evidence for the fix). Original finding, retained verbatim for audit trail: Highest-value finding: none of the 4 MASOC routes gate on isRealUser(authCtx) or check authCtx.authenticated before running — resolveAuthV5's IP-fallback branch sets authenticated:true for every anonymous caller (workers/src/auth/middleware.js's own comment: 'authenticated: true means not-an-invalid-API-key, NOT logged in'), and 30+ other routes in workers/src/index.js do gate on isRealUser (e.g. :2054,3358,4050) including the MFA block immediately above this one (:1917). The only real control on these 4 routes is a 5-runs/min KV rate limiter keyed by user_id/key_id/IP (workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js:176-199, fail-closed if KV is unavailable) — meaning anyone, unauthenticated, can invoke compute-expensive parallel AI-agent orchestration. This directly contradicts a SEPARATE, correctly-gated path to the same capability: workers/src/handlers/aiSecurityCopilot.js:1008,1861-1868,2718 exposes the same functionality as a Copilot tool restricted to PRO/TEAM/ENTERPRISE/MSSP tiers, actively enforced. subscription_gated is recorded as false here because it describes THIS capability's own 4 direct routes; run/stream/dispatch now require a real logged-in principal but still carry no tier/subscription gate of their own (unlike the Copilot-mediated path) — that remains a separate, unfixed gap, not conflated with the auth-gate fix. Two further findings were identified, one now also fixed, one still open: (1) FIXED 2026-07-08, same day as the auth-gate fix, own commit: handleAgentsStream (workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js) hand-rolled its own CORS check instead of using the shared corsHeaders()/withCors() the other 3 routes use, and its check was narrower than the real 6-origin PROD_ORIGINS allowlist (workers/src/middleware/cors.js) — 3 of 6 valid production origins (cyberdudebivash.pages.dev, tools.cyberdudebivash.com, intel.cyberdudebivash.com) had SSE streaming silently rejected by the browser while the JSON/status routes kept working fine from the same origin. Now uses corsHeaders(request, env) directly — see verification.evidence for the fix and its regression coverage. (2) Still open, out of scope for both fixes above (frontend state logic, judged too architecturally consequential to change without confirming intent): frontend/soc-agents.html defaults to selecting all 9 agents including risk_synthesizer (:226-236,269) and both its run paths always send a non-empty agents array (:403,528), so the page's own label text ('all selected = auto-routing by task classifier', :157) is unreachable — classifyTask() is never invoked from this page — and because risk_synthesizer runs once standalone inside the parallel batch (with only CVE/KEV/IOC context, contradicting its own system prompt's expectation of other agents' findings) AND again correctly via the separate synthesis step, every default run burns an extra AI call on a low-value duplicate card. Minor/cosmetic: handleAgentsStatus (:697-702) checks only 4 of the 6 env keys workers/src/core/aiProviderRouter.js actually routes through (missing TOGETHER_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY), understating the 'X/4 providers active' stat — Groq/DeepSeek/CF AI (the 3 the UI copy names) are all genuinely called via real HTTP requests, confirmed in workers/src/core/aiProviderRouter.js.
